reviews_data.review_text:
Goldenkrush tangerine
The best ever
Also the Tacos were very taste

First, the food was excellent. We were there for $1 wing night, and trust, these are some of the best smoked wings I have ever had. My only complaint is that you order sauces, and the wings don’t come sauced, you have to dip the wings in the sauce, which is awkward to eat, especially if sharing, so make sure you ask for individual sauces. We also ordered the hotdog for our daughter, it comes with fries, but they happily substituted it for Mac & cheese, which was good as well. If I was an adult ordering this Mac & Cheese, it could easily be elevated if they added a crispy cheese and bread crumb top layer.

Second, I went to a brewery and felt compelled to talk about the food first, so that is why it is a 4 star and not a 5 star. The beer was okay, but the wonkiness of the service and the absolutely uncomfortable low top chairs, made this a place not top of the list for breweries in the area. I am not surprised by counter service only at a brewery, but I am surprised when I visit a brewery with a restaurant menu then for them to only provide counter service. Once you get your food, counter service makes it inconvenient having to walk away from your party and food to wait in a line at the counter to order your next beer. They need to have a digital solution, where you can order beers through an app, or have someone that walks around and clears glasses and asks if the table needs anymore beer.

I was really impressed that the chef came out of the kitchen to ask us about our meal and gave us some insight on the menu and how the food is prepared. Now imagine if the brewery had someone walking around talking to guests and informing them of the beer options, flavors, and upcoming seasonal batches. What a difference that would have made. But also, those low top table chairs are garbage, I cannot stress that enough. Will we come back to Lost Worlds? maybe…, those wings were so darn good, and now I want to try other menu items.

Ps. We have a 5 year old, so simply moving to a high top table for comfort is not a great option.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you Robert for your kind words. We appreciate you!
reviews_data.review_text: My wife and I were at the Christmas Market on Sunday, 12/21 at the Cornelius location. Once again, Lost Worlds hit it out of the ball park. The brewery was a bit crowded although that didn't effect the impeccable customer service. All of the servers were prompt, courteous, friendly and knowledgeable about each beer being served. The beer selection was phenomenal. The Brewers certainly understand flavor and creation of Lost Worlds beer. I enjoy all dark beer, especially stouts and Porters. On this occasion, I had the German Chocolate Cake and Hansa Bock. Both were OUTSTANDING.  I certainly can understand how the Chocolate Cake received GABF awards. Such detailed flavor that all meshed well. The entertainment selected was very good. Both singers performed well and the music they chose was consistent with what I believe the audience liked. The brewery was very clean and the atmosphere was great. I've been to a number of breweries in the Charlotte Metro and I submit that Lost Worlds is one of the top five. If you want to visit a brewery with outstanding customer service, excellent beer and a very good atmosphere, I highly recommend Lost Worlds Brewery.

reviews_data.review_text:
We hosted our daughter’s first birthday at Lost Worlds Brewing, and it was a great experience from start to finish. The team was incredibly flexible and easy to work with during the planning process, which made everything stress free for us parents.

The staff was kind, helpful, and accommodating throughout the event, and the private space was absolutely perfect for a small celebration—plenty of room, great setup, and a fun atmosphere for both adults and kids.

We couldn’t have asked for a better spot to celebrate. Highly recommend Lost Worlds Brewing to anyone looking to rent a private space for an event.

Was very excited to come here last night with the husband. We are new to the area and wanted to find a yummy place where we could reliably enjoy our Friday nights. I ordered wings and he a burger with fries. Right off the bat I was put off by the presentation of the food. Served in what is clearly the cheapest styrofoam and paper basket they could find. The burger was hideous. The patty tasted worse than those frozen hamburgers at Costco. It was the lowest effort I have ever seen put into a dish. For the $15 we spent on the burger my husband would’ve been happier spending half of that at the cookout next door. The wings were smokey (as advertised by the waitress) but somehow still dry. The $5 fries were below average crinkle cuts. They charged us an additional 50¢ for a nearly empty cup of sauce that came with a melted lid. Don’t even get me started on the beer. My husband is an avid beer drinker so our main reasons for coming to these places are the brews. The IPAs he had were so mediocre. Stout was undrinkable. I have never seen my husband look so disgusted while trying a new drink. It’s clear the brewer doesn’t understand beer beyond light lagers. This place clearly focused on style over substance. Giving two stars only for the ambiance and the live music.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Garrett - First off, thank you for your feedback and for being a guest at Lost Worlds. I'm extremely sorry you had to be witness to what transpired last night at Lost Worlds. We never want our guests to have a bad experience and be left with a negative impression. For whatever reason last night, we had a large number of kids in the biergarten as well as two events going on. Unfortunately, many of the kids were note being supervised and were running through a rehearsal dinner. Additionally, they were throwing items and running through the back parking lot. We have at least two reports of a kid almost getting hit because they ran in front of a car. Our guest services model was built to always focus first on the safety of our guests. We drop everything to address situations when someone may get hurt. While we want our guests to have a memorable experience, we also want them to arrive safe in their beds at the end of the night. After safety, we focus on comfort. Several individuals were hit by items thrown by kids or were run into by kids. The bride-to-be had wine spilled on her by a running child. When these situations occur, our Adventure Guides get down to eye level with the kids and ask them to refrain from whatever they are doing. We watch things unfold, and then we speak to the parents. If it continues, our team huddles and assesses the situation for options including moving the kids to a part of the facility,  discussing alternatives with the parents, or asking them to leave. In this instance, the team opted to discuss it with the parents again... and then a fourth time... and a fifth. The parents were unresponsive. Despite our best efforts, the kids did not behave. During this time, you would not have seen it but we had over a dozen complaints. Our General Manager became involved and had to be more firm with the parents than we would prefer. I know this impacted you in a negative manner - but he also received several notes of thanks. My email this morning had three emails commending his actions to myself and how he addressed the situation. Again, you may have seen or heard something they didn't. I just want you to know that our guests are the most important part of our business. We want them to have a memorable experience at Lost Worlds - but only after we know they are safe and comfortable. If you wish to discuss this incident further, you can reach me at adventureguide@lostworldsbeer.com. I hope you return to visit us again so you will understand how rare we have situations like this. Cheers!

He caused such a scene twice that a couple (who later told me they go there every week and had no kids) tried to stick up for the kids & calm the GM down and he then started arguing with them while everyone outside watched in shock. reviews_data.review_text: Spacious and open place with lots of seating indoors and out. Plenty of knowledgeable and friendly staff to take libation orders. The beer tastes great with a little something for every beer connoisseur: light, lager, ale, IPA, cider, sours... there's even some good wine selections. A food truck rotation scheduled outside to feed your appetite. There's also several restaurants located in the plaza nearby. Tonight's truck was Muchachos Tacos. Themed flights with the Adventurers's flight affording you a chance to earn a koozie by answering a trivia question derived from artifacts around the brewery. Great fun. Outside stage features different bands and soloists throughout the month. There's fun events slated different nights of the week. Good parking, too. We had such a good time, that we will definitely return soon.

reviews_data.review_text:
Goldenkrush tangerine
The best ever
Also the Tacos were very taste

First, the food was excellent. We were there for $1 wing night, and trust, these are some of the best smoked wings I have ever had. My only complaint is that you order sauces, and the wings don’t come sauced, you have to dip the wings in the sauce, which is awkward to eat, especially if sharing, so make sure you ask for individual sauces. We also ordered the hotdog for our daughter, it comes with fries, but they happily substituted it for Mac & cheese, which was good as well. If I was an adult ordering this Mac & Cheese, it could easily be elevated if they added a crispy cheese and bread crumb top layer.

Second, I went to a brewery and felt compelled to talk about the food first, so that is why it is a 4 star and not a 5 star. The beer was okay, but the wonkiness of the service and the absolutely uncomfortable low top chairs, made this a place not top of the list for breweries in the area. I am not surprised by counter service only at a brewery, but I am surprised when I visit a brewery with a restaurant menu then for them to only provide counter service. Once you get your food, counter service makes it inconvenient having to walk away from your party and food to wait in a line at the counter to order your next beer. They need to have a digital solution, where you can order beers through an app, or have someone that walks around and clears glasses and asks if the table needs anymore beer.

I was really impressed that the chef came out of the kitchen to ask us about our meal and gave us some insight on the menu and how the food is prepared. Now imagine if the brewery had someone walking around talking to guests and informing them of the beer options, flavors, and upcoming seasonal batches. What a difference that would have made. But also, those low top table chairs are garbage, I cannot stress that enough. Will we come back to Lost Worlds? maybe…, those wings were so darn good, and now I want to try other menu items.

Ps. We have a 5 year old, so simply moving to a high top table for comfort is not a great option.

reviews_data.review_text: My wife and I were at the Christmas Market on Sunday, 12/21 at the Cornelius location. Once again, Lost Worlds hit it out of the ball park. The brewery was a bit crowded although that didn't effect the impeccable customer service. All of the servers were prompt, courteous, friendly and knowledgeable about each beer being served. The beer selection was phenomenal. The Brewers certainly understand flavor and creation of Lost Worlds beer. I enjoy all dark beer, especially stouts and Porters. On this occasion, I had the German Chocolate Cake and Hansa Bock. Both were OUTSTANDING.  I certainly can understand how the Chocolate Cake received GABF awards. Such detailed flavor that all meshed well. The entertainment selected was very good. Both singers performed well and the music they chose was consistent with what I believe the audience liked. The brewery was very clean and the atmosphere was great. I've been to a number of breweries in the Charlotte Metro and I submit that Lost Worlds is one of the top five. If you want to visit a brewery with outstanding customer service, excellent beer and a very good atmosphere, I highly recommend Lost Worlds Brewery.

Was very excited to come here last night with the husband. We are new to the area and wanted to find a yummy place where we could reliably enjoy our Friday nights. I ordered wings and he a burger with fries. Right off the bat I was put off by the presentation of the food. Served in what is clearly the cheapest styrofoam and paper basket they could find. The burger was hideous. The patty tasted worse than those frozen hamburgers at Costco. It was the lowest effort I have ever seen put into a dish. For the $15 we spent on the burger my husband would’ve been happier spending half of that at the cookout next door. The wings were smokey (as advertised by the waitress) but somehow still dry. The $5 fries were below average crinkle cuts. They charged us an additional 50¢ for a nearly empty cup of sauce that came with a melted lid. Don’t even get me started on the beer. My husband is an avid beer drinker so our main reasons for coming to these places are the brews. The IPAs he had were so mediocre. Stout was undrinkable. I have never seen my husband look so disgusted while trying a new drink. It’s clear the brewer doesn’t understand beer beyond light lagers. This place clearly focused on style over substance. Giving two stars only for the ambiance and the live music.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Garrett - First off, thank you for your feedback and for being a guest at Lost Worlds. I'm extremely sorry you had to be witness to what transpired last night at Lost Worlds. We never want our guests to have a bad experience and be left with a negative impression. For whatever reason last night, we had a large number of kids in the biergarten as well as two events going on. Unfortunately, many of the kids were note being supervised and were running through a rehearsal dinner. Additionally, they were throwing items and running through the back parking lot. We have at least two reports of a kid almost getting hit because they ran in front of a car. Our guest services model was built to always focus first on the safety of our guests. We drop everything to address situations when someone may get hurt. While we want our guests to have a memorable experience, we also want them to arrive safe in their beds at the end of the night. After safety, we focus on comfort. Several individuals were hit by items thrown by kids or were run into by kids. The bride-to-be had wine spilled on her by a running child. When these situations occur, our Adventure Guides get down to eye level with the kids and ask them to refrain from whatever they are doing. We watch things unfold, and then we speak to the parents. If it continues, our team huddles and assesses the situation for options including moving the kids to a part of the facility,  discussing alternatives with the parents, or asking them to leave. In this instance, the team opted to discuss it with the parents again... and then a fourth time... and a fifth. The parents were unresponsive. Despite our best efforts, the kids did not behave. During this time, you would not have seen it but we had over a dozen complaints. Our General Manager became involved and had to be more firm with the parents than we would prefer. I know this impacted you in a negative manner - but he also received several notes of thanks. My email this morning had three emails commending his actions to myself and how he addressed the situation. Again, you may have seen or heard something they didn't. I just want you to know that our guests are the most important part of our business. We want them to have a memorable experience at Lost Worlds - but only after we know they are safe and comfortable. If you wish to discuss this incident further, you can reach me at adventureguide@lostworldsbeer.com. I hope you return to visit us again so you will understand how rare we have situations like this. Cheers!

The staff's attentiveness and the exceptional selection of beers greatly contributed to the event’s ambiance. I'm thankful for their dedication to supporting community events and their role in making my Friendsgiving memorable.

I highly recommend Lost Worlds Brewery for anyone seeking a great venue. Their commitment to quality and service is truly commendable. A huge thank you to Lost Worlds Brewery for a successful collaboration! Looking forward to more collaborations in the future!

reviews_data.review_text: Spacious and open place with lots of seating indoors and out. Plenty of knowledgeable and friendly staff to take libation orders. The beer tastes great with a little something for every beer connoisseur: light, lager, ale, IPA, cider, sours... there's even some good wine selections. A food truck rotation scheduled outside to feed your appetite. There's also several restaurants located in the plaza nearby. Tonight's truck was Muchachos Tacos. Themed flights with the Adventurers's flight affording you a chance to earn a koozie by answering a trivia question derived from artifacts around the brewery. Great fun. Outside stage features different bands and soloists throughout the month. There's fun events slated different nights of the week. Good parking, too. We had such a good time, that we will definitely return soon.

reviews_data.review_text: Really cool venue! It’s an open floor concept with an industrial vibe. Lots of seating and very clean. Friendly and helpful staff. They were so many beers on the menu that we wanted to try!! Thanks to staff recommendation, we decided to order two flights (4 4-oz pours each) ranging from lagers to IPAs. Each beer was unique and delicious! As a note, we came during off hours (230pm on a Wednesday) but we checked the event calendar, and they have live music, trivia, yoga, and a variety of food options throughout the week!! Definitely a must try!

reviews_data.review_text: I can not say enough about Lost Worlds and their staff.   We hired out their private event space for our son's wedding welcome drinks.   Lily the event manager was amazing from when we first talked to her to the night of the event.   She accommodated every single request - from a slide show to letting us bring in some appetizers/food.   And let me just say the night of the event - the staff completely took care of our guests.    They took drink orders quickly and correctly - even though some of our guests had wandered out of the private room.   We had been to the brewery several times before but they truly exceeded our expectations.  Do not hesitate to book this private space.  We certainly will when the occasion arises again.

As I sit here, I can’t help but contemplate my life. I could have been an engineer. I could have been a doctor. I could have been a somebody..

But in the end, it doesn’t matter, because
I guess my life isn’t so bad, and the staff makes me feel like I’m important.

Thank you Lost World, for filing my belly and making me happy.

So many establishments are so quick to jump on the virtue signaling bandwagon and forget where they came from.
